2603.11721.title: When OpenClaw Meets Hospital: Toward an Agentic Operating System for Dynamic Clinical Workflows
2603.11721.paper_summary: Large language model (LLM) agents extend conventional generative models by integrating reasoning, tool invocation, and persistent memory. Recent studies suggest that such agents may significantly improve clinical workflows by automating documentation, coordinating care processes, and assisting medical decision making. However, despite rapid progress, deploying autonomous agents in healthcare environments remains difficult due to reliability limitations, security risks, and insufficient long-term memory mechanisms. This work proposes an architecture that adapts LLM agents for hospital environments. The design introduces four core components: a restricted execution environment inspired by Linux multi-user systems, a document-centric interaction paradigm connecting patient and clinician agents, a page-indexed memory architecture designed for long-term clinical context management, and a curated medical skills library enabling ad-hoc composition of clinical task sequences. Rather than granting agents unrestricted system access, the architecture constrains actions through predefined skill interfaces and resource isolation. We argue that such a system forms the basis of an Agentic Operating System for Hospital, a computing layer capable of coordinating clinical workflows while maintaining safety, transparency, and auditability. This work grounds the design in OpenClaw, an open-source autonomous agent framework that structures agent capabilities as a curated library of discrete skills, and extends it with the infrastructure-level constraints required for safe clinical deployment.
2603.11721.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLM）代理擴展了傳統生成模型，通過整合推理、工具調用和持久記憶。最近的研究表明，這些代理可能通過自動化文檔、協調護理流程和協助醫療決策顯著改善臨床工作流程。然而，儘管進展迅速，在醫療環境中部署自主代理仍然困難，原因在於可靠性限制、安全風險和長期記憶機制不足。本研究提出了一種適應醫院環境的LLM代理架構。該設計引入了四個核心組件：受限執行環境，靈感來自Linux多用戶系統；以文檔為中心的互動範式，連接患者和臨床醫生代理；設計用於長期臨床上下文管理的頁面索引記憶架構；以及一個策劃的醫療技能庫，使臨床任務序列的臨時組合成為可能。該架構通過預定義的技能介面和資源隔離來限制行動，而不是授予代理無限制的系統訪問權限。我們認為這樣的系統構成了醫院的代理操作系統的基礎，這是一個能夠協調臨床工作流程的計算層，同時保持安全性、透明性和可審計性。本研究將設計基於OpenClaw，一個開源自主代理框架，該框架將代理能力結構化為策劃的離散技能庫，並擴展了安全臨床部署所需的基礎設施級約束。
2603.11721.author: Wenxian Yang et.al.
2603.11721.authors: Wenxian Yang, Hanzheng Qiu, Bangqun Zhang, Chengquan Li, Zhiyong Huang, Xiaobin Feng, Rongshan Yu, Jiahong Dong
